case_title: MILDRED NIDDS et al VS. PRINCESS CRUISES

docket_entries.description: SERVICE OF COMPLAINT BY CERTIFIED MAIL FILED BY PLAINTIFF NIDDS, MILDRED AS TO DEFENDANT PRINCESS CRUISES
MONEY CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF FOR $3,700.00 FILED BY NIDDS, MILDRED NIDDS, RAYMOND AS TO DEFENDANT PRINCESS CRUISES ORDER SETTING TRIAL ISSUED FOR JUN-12-2009 AT 03:30 PM IN DEPT 506
PROOF OF SERVICE FILED ON CLAIM OF PLAINTIFF SERVED APR-21-2009, COURT CERTIFIED MAIL RETURNED SERVED AS TO DEFENDANT PRINCESS CRUISES
CORRESPONDENCE FROM DEFENDANT PRINCESS CRUISES, FILED. CORRESPONDENCE REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL AND CHALLENGE OF VENUE.
SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF JUN-12-2009 - JUDGMENT. (CLERK: V. MU IN ROOM 103)
STIPULATION FOR HEARING BEFORE PRO TEM COMMISSIONER FILED BY NIDDS, MILDRED NIDDS, RAYMOND PRINCESS CRUISES

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF NIDDS, MILDRED NIDDS, RAYMOND IS NOT OWED ANY MONEY BY DEFENDANT PRINCESS CRUISES THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS BASED ON VENUE. THE COURT FINDS PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE APPLICABLE SATATUTES OF LIMITATION. EVEN IF THE CLAIM WERE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTES OF LIMITATION, DEFENDANT WOULD PREVAIL AS PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN BY A PREPONDERENCE OF THE EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT WAS NEGLIGENT, THAT SUCH ALLEGED NEGLIGENCE WAS THE CAUSE OF PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES, OR THAT DEFENDANT BREACHED ITS CONTRACT WITH PLAINTIFFS. STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED
DECLARATION OF APPEARANCE ON BEHALF OF CORPORATION PURSUANT TO CCP 116.540 (B)-(H) FILED BY DEFENDANT PRINCESS CRUISES

SMALL CLAIMS CALENDAR OF JUN-12-2009 - TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS OF JUN-12-2009 BY JUDGE PRO TEM: MARK SCHREIBER. APPEARANCES BY BOTH PARTIES. EXHIBITS RETAINED. CLERK: S. VANTREE. NOT REPORTED. (506)
EXHIBITS FROM EXHIBIT REPORT DATED NOV-02-2009 ORDERED DESTROYED
